title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Lyn discusses the late Gordon Claude with her mother, Adela Marchmont. Adela describes Gordon’s widow, Rosaline (also known as Mrs. Andeh), as a neurotic and clumsy woman who suffered trauma following an air raid. Rosaline is currently residing in Frognack with her brother, David Hunter, an Irish man whom Adela characterizes as rude and impolite. Adela expresses suspicion regarding Rosaline’s motives, noting rumors that she traveled from South America during the war specifically to find a wealthy husband.

Adela reveals that Gordon’s marriage to Rosaline invalidated his 1940 will, which had previously secured the family's financial future. As a result, Rosaline inherited his entire estate. This leaves Adela in significant financial distress; despite Gordon's prior promises to provide for the family, Adela is facing an overdrawn bank account and mounting bills for house repairs and war damage insurance. While Lyn suggests that Rosaline has a moral obligation to assist them, Adela refuses to ask for favors, particularly given the potential influence of Rosaline's brother.

In a separate setting in Wormsley Valley, Francis Claude, a forty-eight-year-old woman capable of managing servants, observes her husband, Jeremy Claude. During their meal, Francis notices that Jeremy, sixty-three, appears unusually expressionless, a change that she immediately detects. A fifteen-year-old girl serves them, appearing highly sensitive to Francis's reactions.
